standards.description: Read and write six-digit numerals
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will read, write, and identify the place and value of each digit in a six-digit numeral, with and without models.

standards.description: Compare and order whole numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will compare and order whole numbers, each 9,999 or less.

standards.description: Represent fractions and mixed numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will name and write fractions and mixed numbers represented by a model.

standards.description: Estimate and determine sums and differences
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will estimate solutions to and solve single-step and multistep problems involving the sum or difference of two whole numbers, each 9,999 or less, with or without regrouping.

standards.description: Read, write, and identify place value to millions
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will read, write, and identify the place and value of each digit in a nine-digit whole number.

standards.description: Compare and order whole numbers to millions
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will compare and order whole numbers expressed through millions.

standards.description: Read, write, represent, and identify decimals
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will read, write, represent, and identify decimals expressed through thousandths.

standards.description: Compare and order fractions and mixed numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will compare and order fractions and mixed numbers, with and without models.

standards.description: Estimate and solve problems with whole numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will estimate and solve single-step and multistep addition, subtraction, and multiplication problems with whole numbers.

standards.description: Determine prime and composite numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will determine whether a whole number from 2 to 100 is prime or composite.

standards.description: Represent and identify equivalencies among fractions
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will represent and identify equivalencies among fractions and decimals, with and without models.

standards.description: Represent relationships between quantities using ratios
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will represent relationships between quantities using ratios, and will use appropriate notations, such as a/b, a to b, and a:b.

standards.description: Represent and determine equivalencies among fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, and percents
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will represent and determine equivalencies among fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, and percents.

standards.description: Identify and represent integers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will identify and represent integers; order and compare integers; and identify and describe absolute value of integers.

standards.description: Recognize and represent patterns with whole number exponents and perfect squares
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will recognize and represent patterns with whole number exponents and perfect squares.

standards.description: Investigate and describe the concept of positive exponents and perfect squares
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and describe the concept of positive exponents and perfect squares.

standards.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide integers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will add, subtract, multiply, and divide integers.

standards.description: Investigate and describe the concept of negative exponents for powers of ten
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and describe the concept of negative exponents for powers of ten; compare and order numbers greater than zero written in scientific notation.

standards.description: Solve practical problems involving operations with rational numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve practical problems involving operations with rational numbers.

standards.description: Solve single-step and multistep problems using proportional reasoning
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve single-step and multistep practical problems, using proportional reasoning.

standards.description: Solve problems involving percent increase and decrease
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve single-step and multistep practical problems, using proportional reasoning: problems involving tips, tax, and percent increase and decrease.

standards.description: Compare and order real numbers
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will compare and order real numbers.

standards.description: Describe the relationships between the subsets of the real number system
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will describe the relationships between the subsets of the real number system.

standards.description: Estimate and determine the two consecutive integers between which a square root lies
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will estimate and determine the two consecutive integers between which a square root lies.

standards.description: Solve practical problems involving consumer applications
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve practical problems involving consumer applications.

standards.description: Evaluate algebraic expressions
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will evaluate an algebraic expression for given replacement values of the variables.

standards.description: Solve multistep linear equations in one variable
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve multistep linear equations in one variable with the variable on one or both sides of the equation.

standards.description: Solve multistep linear inequalities in one variable
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve multistep linear inequalities in one variable with the variable on one or both sides of the inequality.

standards.description: Develop an understanding of scientific investigation
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations.

standards.description: Investigate and understand simple machines
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that objects can be described by their physical properties. Key concepts include simple machines and their uses.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the water cycle
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that ecosystems support a diversity of plants and animals that share limited resources. The water cycle is a key process.

standards.description: Demonstrate understanding of scientific investigation
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations.

standards.description: Investigate and understand electricity
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the characteristics of electricity and its application in circuits.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the organization of the solar system
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the organization of the solar system, including the sun, moon, Earth, and other planets.

standards.description: Plan and conduct investigations
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations.

standards.description: Investigate and understand matter and its properties
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that matter is anything that has mass and takes up space. Key concepts include atoms and elements.

standards.description: Investigate and understand Earth's surface
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand how Earth's surface is constantly changing. Key concepts include weathering, erosion, and deposition.

standards.description: Demonstrate understanding of scientific reasoning
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations.

standards.description: Investigate and understand basic sources of energy
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand basic sources of energy, their origins, transformations, and uses.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the unique properties of water
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the unique properties and characteristics of water and its roles in the natural and human-made environment.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the natural processes and human interactions that affect watershed systems
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the natural processes and human interactions that affect watershed systems.

standards.description: Plan and conduct investigations
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations in which data are collected and recorded.

standards.description: Investigate and understand cellular organization
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that all living things are composed of cells. Key concepts include cell structure and organelles.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the relationships among organisms in biological communities
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the relationships among organisms in biological communities. Key concepts include producer, consumer, decomposer, predator, prey.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the Earth's history and geologic processes
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the Earth's crust undergoes change as a result of geologic processes.

standards.description: Plan and conduct investigations using the scientific method
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations using experimental design.

standards.description: Investigate and understand force, motion, and energy relationships
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the relationships among force, motion, and energy. Key concepts include speed, velocity, and acceleration.

standards.description: Investigate and understand atoms and the periodic table
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the organization of the periodic table. Key concepts include atomic structure, elements, and compounds.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the law of conservation of matter and energy
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the law of conservation of matter and energy applies to all transformations of matter and energy.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts, including narratives and poetry.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of nonfiction texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of nonfiction texts.

standards.description: Write for a variety of purposes
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write for a variety of purposes, including to describe, to inform, to entertain, and to explain.

standards.description: Expand vocabulary when reading
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will expand vocabulary when reading, using context clues, knowledge of roots, affixes, synonyms, and antonyms.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts, including narratives and poetry, identifying the author's purpose, making inferences, and drawing conclusions.

standards.description: Write effectively in a variety of forms
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write effectively in a variety of forms, including narrative, descriptive, opinion, and expository.

standards.description: Expand vocabulary when reading
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will expand vocabulary when reading, using knowledge of roots, affixes, synonyms, antonyms, and homophones.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts, including narratives and poetry, identifying theme, point of view, and figurative language.

standards.description: Write for a variety of purposes
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write for a variety of purposes: to describe, to inform, to entertain, to explain, and to persuade.

standards.description: Read and learn meanings of unfamiliar words
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and learn the meanings of unfamiliar words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing word structure, and consulting reference materials.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of a variety of fictional texts, narrative nonfiction, and poetry, describing how the choice of language, setting, characters, and point of view contributes to the author's style.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of nonfiction texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of a variety of nonfiction texts, identifying main idea, supporting details, and organizational patterns.

standards.description: Write in a variety of forms
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write in a variety of forms, including narrative, expository, persuasive, and reflective, with an emphasis on narrative and reflective writing.

standards.description: Read and learn meanings of unfamiliar words
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read to determine the meanings and pronunciations of unfamiliar words and phrases within authentic texts.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and demonstrate comprehension of a variety of fictional texts, narrative nonfiction, and poetry, comparing and contrasting various forms and genres.

standards.description: Write in a variety of forms
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write in a variety of forms with an emphasis on exposition, persuasion, and narration.

standards.description: Apply knowledge of word origins and derivations
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will apply knowledge of word origins, derivations, and figurative language to extend vocabulary development in authentic texts.

standards.description: Read and demonstrate comprehension of fictional texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and analyze a variety of fictional texts, narrative nonfiction, and poetry, making inferences and drawing conclusions based on explicit and implied information.

standards.description: Write in a variety of forms
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write in a variety of forms, including narrative, expository, persuasive, and reflective.

standards.description: Represent verbal quantitative situations algebraically
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will represent verbal quantitative situations algebraically and evaluate these expressions for given replacement values of the variables.

standards.description: Perform operations on polynomials
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will perform operations on polynomials, including adding, subtracting, multiplying, dividing, and factoring.

standards.description: Solve multistep linear and quadratic equations
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve multistep linear and quadratic equations in two variables.

standards.description: Graph linear equations and linear inequalities in two variables
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will graph linear equations and linear inequalities in two variables.

standards.description: Investigate and analyze linear and quadratic function families
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and analyze linear and quadratic function families and their characteristics.

standards.description: Use deductive reasoning to construct and judge validity of a logical argument
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will construct and judge the validity of a logical argument consisting of a set of premises and a conclusion, using deductive reasoning.

standards.description: Solve problems involving symmetry and transformation
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student will solve problems involving symmetry and transformation, including translation, reflection, rotation, and dilation.

standards.description: Prove triangles congruent
standards.subject: Mathematics
standards.full_text: The student, given information concerning the lengths of sides and/or measures of angles in triangles, will solve problems, including practical problems, and prove two triangles are congruent.

standards.description: Plan and conduct investigations using the scientific method
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific reasoning, logic, and the nature of science by planning and conducting investigations.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the chemical and biochemical principles essential for life
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the chemical and biochemical principles essential for life, including water chemistry, organic molecules, and the role of enzymes.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the relationships between cell structure and function
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand relationships between cell structure and function, including organelle function and cell division.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the mechanisms of heredity
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the role of nucleic acids, DNA, and RNA, in the mechanisms of heredity, including the genetic code and protein synthesis.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the theory of biological evolution
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand bases for the modern classification of organisms, including evidence of evolution through comparative studies.

standards.description: Investigate and understand dynamic equilibria within populations, communities, and ecosystems
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand dynamic equilibria within populations, communities, and ecosystems, including nutrient cycling, succession, and biodiversity.

standards.description: Apply knowledge of word origins, derivations, and figurative language
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will apply knowledge of word origins, derivations, and figurative language to extend vocabulary development in authentic texts.

standards.description: Read, comprehend, and analyze literary texts of different cultures and eras
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read, comprehend, and analyze literary texts of different cultures and eras, analyzing the development of themes across texts.

standards.description: Read, comprehend, and analyze literary texts
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read, comprehend, and analyze literary texts of different cultures and eras, comparing and contrasting literary elements across texts.

standards.description: Read and analyze American literature
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will read and analyze relationships among American literature, history, and culture.

standards.description: Write, revise, and edit in a variety of forms
standards.subject: English
standards.full_text: The student will write, revise, and edit personal, professional, and informational compositions, synthesizing information from multiple sources.

standards.description: Investigate and understand scientific reasoning and logic
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific and engineering practices by planning and conducting investigations in which observations are made and are repeated to ensure accuracy; predictions and hypotheses are formulated based on cause-and-effect relationships; data are collected, recorded, analyzed, and reported using metric measurements and tools; models and simulations are designed and used to illustrate and explain phenomena; and current applications are used to reinforce life science concepts.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that all living things are composed of cells
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that all living things are composed of one or more cells that support life processes, as described by the cell theory.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that living things show patterns of cellular organization
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that living things show patterns of cellular organization. Key ideas include cells, tissues, organs, and systems; life functions are carried out within the cell and result from the complementary activity of organelles.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that the basic needs of organisms must be met
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the basic needs of organisms must be met through interactions with the environment. Key ideas include energy is transferred through food webs; matter is recycled; and living systems are interdependent.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the interrelationships among populations in a community
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that biotic and abiotic factors affect an ecosystem. Key ideas include interactions among populations including competition, predation, parasitism, mutualism, and commensalism; the role of niche; and the effects of change on ecosystems.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that organisms within an ecosystem are dependent on one another and on nonliving components
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that organisms within an ecosystem are dependent on one another and on nonliving components of the environment. Key ideas include the carbon, water, and nitrogen cycles; ecosystems, communities, populations, and organisms; and habitat and niche.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that organisms can be classified
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that organisms can be classified based on shared characteristics. Key ideas include the distinguishing characteristics of kingdoms; the major animal and plant phyla; and the use of classification systems.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that ecosystems, communities, populations, and organisms are dynamic
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that ecosystems, communities, populations, and organisms are dynamic and change over time. Key ideas include how organisms respond to daily, seasonal, and long-term changes; and how populations change through time.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that cells reproduce
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the basic unit of life is the cell and that cells reproduce. Key ideas include cell division (mitosis) produces two identical daughter cells; the cell cycle; and cell division is important for growth, repair, and reproduction.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that organisms reproduce and transmit genetic information
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that organisms reproduce and transmit genetic information to new generations. Key ideas include DNA and genes; the role of meiosis; Mendelian genetics; and the impact of mutations.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that organisms change over time
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that organisms change over time. Key ideas include evidence of evolution including the fossil record, comparative anatomy, and DNA analysis; how natural selection leads to adaptation; and Charles Darwin's role in developing the theory of evolution.

standards.description: Demonstrate understanding of scientific and engineering practices
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific and engineering practices by planning and carrying out investigations, interpreting and analyzing data, constructing and critiquing conclusions, developing and using models, and obtaining, evaluating, and communicating information.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that matter is composed of atoms
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that matter is composed of atoms. Key ideas include the understanding of atoms has developed over time; the periodic table can be used to predict the chemical and physical properties of matter; and the kinetic molecular theory describes the states of matter.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that matter has properties and is conserved
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that matter has properties and is conserved in chemical and physical processes. Key ideas include pure substances can be identified by their physical and chemical properties; physical changes and chemical changes occur in matter; ionic and covalent bonding can be used to explain the properties of a compound; and balanced equations model the conservation of matter in chemical reactions.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the periodic table as a model for organizing elements
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the periodic table is a model used to organize elements based on their atomic structure. Key ideas include symbols, atomic numbers, atomic mass, groups or families, and periods are identified on the periodic table; and elements are classified as metals, metalloids, and nonmetals.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that energy is conserved
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that energy is conserved. Key ideas include energy can be stored in different ways; energy is transferred and transformed; and energy can be transformed to meet the needs of society.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that waves are important in the movement of energy
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that waves are important in the movement of energy. Key ideas include longitudinal and transverse waves have different characteristics; mechanical waves need a medium to transfer energy; waves can interact; and energy associated with waves has many practical applications.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the characteristics of electromagnetic radiation
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the characteristics of transverse waves. Key ideas include the characteristics of electromagnetic radiation, including visible light; and the regions of the electromagnetic spectrum.

standards.description: Investigate and understand work, force, and motion
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the scientific principles of work, force, and motion. Key ideas include motion can be described using position and time; and Newton's laws of motion explain the interaction between force, mass, and motion.

standards.description: Investigate and understand electricity and magnetism
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand the basic principles of electricity and magnetism. Key ideas include an imbalance of charge generates static electricity; materials have different conductive properties; electric circuits transfer energy; magnetic fields are related to the motion of electric charges; and technologies have been developed based on the principles of electricity and magnetism.

standards.description: Demonstrate understanding of scientific and engineering practices
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific and engineering practices by planning and carrying out investigations, interpreting and analyzing data, constructing and critiquing conclusions, developing and using models, and obtaining, evaluating, and communicating information.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the origin and evolution of the universe
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that scientific concepts related to the origin and evolution of the universe are based on physical evidence. Key ideas include the Big Bang theory proposes the formation of the universe; stars and galaxies form and change over time; the sun, planets, moons, asteroids, and comets have characteristics and properties; and space exploration continues to provide evidence about the universe.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that Earth is unique in its ability to support life
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that Earth is unique in our solar system in its ability to support life. Key ideas include Earth's proximity to the sun and its relationship to the water cycle support life; and the sun-Earth-moon system causes seasons, tides, and eclipses.

standards.description: Investigate and understand rock-forming and ore minerals
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that major rock-forming and ore minerals have specific physical and chemical properties that make them useful. Key ideas include physical and chemical properties are used to identify and classify minerals; mineral characteristics determine how they are used; and rock-forming minerals are formed in specific ways.

standards.description: Investigate and understand rock transformation and the rock cycle
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that igneous, metamorphic, and sedimentary rocks can transform from one to another. Key ideas include Earth's materials are finite and can be transformed into different forms; the rock cycle models the transformation of rocks; rock layers have specific properties; and plate tectonic and surface processes transform Earth materials.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that resource use is complex
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that resource use is complex. Key ideas include global resource use has environmental liabilities and benefits; availability, renewal rates, and economic impact are considerations when using resources; use of Virginia resources has an impact on the environment and the economy; and the selection of various energy sources has environmental and economic impacts.

standards.description: Investigate and understand plate tectonic theory
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that plate tectonic theory explains Earth's internal and external geologic processes. Key ideas include convection currents in Earth's interior lead to the movement of plates, creation of the magnetic field, and the distribution of materials in Earth's layers; features and processes occur within plates and at plate boundaries; interaction between tectonic plates causes the development of mountain ranges and ocean basins; and evidence of geologic processes is found in Virginia's geologic landscape.

standards.description: Investigate and understand freshwater resources
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that freshwater resources influence and are influenced by geologic processes and activities of humans. Key ideas include water impacts geologic processes including soil development and karst topography; the nature of materials in the subsurface affect the water table and future availability of fresh water; weather and human usage impact freshwater resources; and stream processes and dynamics impact the major watershed systems in Virginia, including the Chesapeake Bay and its tributaries.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the history and evolution of Earth through rocks and fossils
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that many aspects of the history and evolution of Earth and life can be inferred by studying rocks and fossils. Key ideas include traces and remains of ancient life are preserved in sedimentary rocks; superposition, cross-cutting relationships, index fossils, and radioactive decay are methods of dating rocks; absolute and relative dating have different applications but can be used together; and rocks and fossils from many different geologic periods and epochs are found in Virginia.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that oceans are complex, dynamic systems
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that oceans are complex, dynamic systems and are subject to long- and short-term variations. Key ideas include chemical, biological, and physical changes impact the oceans; environmental and geologic occurrences affect ocean dynamics; unevenly distributed heat in the oceans drives much of Earth's weather; features of the sea floor reflect tectonic and other geological processes; and human actions impact oceans and the coastal zone including the Chesapeake Bay.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that the atmosphere is a complex, dynamic system
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the atmosphere is a complex, dynamic system and is subject to long- and short-term variations. Key ideas include the composition of the atmosphere is critical to most forms of life; biologic and geologic interactions over long and short time spans change atmospheric composition; natural events and human actions may stress atmospheric regulation mechanisms; and human actions, including economic and policy decisions, impact the atmosphere.

standards.description: Investigate and understand weather and climate
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that Earth's weather and climate are the result of the interaction of the sun's energy with the atmosphere, oceans, and the land. Key ideas include weather involves the reflection, absorption, storage, and redistribution of energy; weather patterns can be predicted based on changes in current conditions; extreme imbalances in energy distribution may lead to severe weather conditions; models based on current conditions are used to predict weather phenomena; and changes in the atmosphere and the oceans due to human activity affect global climate.

standards.description: Demonstrate understanding of scientific and engineering practices
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific and engineering practices by planning and carrying out investigations, interpreting and analyzing data, constructing and critiquing conclusions, developing and using models, and obtaining, evaluating, and communicating information.

standards.description: Investigate and understand atomic structure and the periodic table
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that elements have properties based on their atomic structure. The periodic table is an organizational tool for elements based on these properties. Key information includes average atomic mass, isotopes, mass number, and atomic number; nuclear decay; trends within groups and periods; electron configurations, valence electrons, excited electrons, and ions; and historical and quantum models.

standards.description: Investigate and understand chemical reactions and bonding
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that atoms are conserved in chemical reactions. Key ideas include chemical formulas are models used to represent the number of each type of atom in a substance; substances are named based on the number of atoms and the type of interactions between atoms; balanced chemical equations model rearrangement of atoms; atoms bond based on electron interactions; molecular geometry is predictive of physical and chemical properties; and reaction types can be predicted and classified.

standards.description: Investigate and understand molar relationships
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that molar relationships compare and predict chemical quantities. Key ideas include Avogadro's principle is the basis for molar relationships; and stoichiometry mathematically describes quantities in chemical composition and in chemical reactions.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that solutions behave in predictable ways
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that solutions behave in predictable and quantifiable ways. Key ideas include molar relationships determine solution concentration; changes in temperature can affect solubility; extent of dissociation defines types of electrolytes; pH and pOH quantify acid and base dissociation; and colligative properties depend on the extent of dissociation.

standards.description: Investigate and understand phases of matter and kinetic molecular theory
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the phases of matter are explained by the kinetic molecular theory. Key ideas include pressure and temperature define the phase of a substance; properties of ideal gases are described by gas laws; and intermolecular forces affect physical properties.

standards.description: Investigate and understand thermodynamics
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that thermodynamics explains the relationship between matter and energy. Key ideas include heat energy affects matter and interactions of matter; heating curves provide information about a substance; reactions are endothermic or exothermic; energy changes in reactions occur as bonds are broken and formed; collision theory predicts the rate of reactions; rates of reactions depend on catalysts and activation energy; and enthalpy and entropy determine the extent of a reaction.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that bacteria and viruses affect living systems
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that bacteria and viruses have an effect on living systems. Key ideas include viruses depend on a host organism; modes of reproduction and replication are compared; structures and functions are compared; the role of bacteria and viruses in the environment is assessed; and germ theory is supported by scientific evidence.

standards.description: Investigate and understand modern classification systems
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that modern classification systems can be used as organizational tools. Key ideas include structural and biochemical similarities and differences are used to classify organisms; the fossil record provides evidence of relationships; developmental stages reveal similarities; Archaea, Bacteria, and Eukarya are the three domains of life; and the functions and processes of protists, fungi, plants, and animals are compared.

standards.description: Demonstrate understanding of scientific and engineering practices
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will demonstrate an understanding of scientific and engineering practices by asking questions and defining problems, planning and carrying out investigations, interpreting and analyzing data, constructing and critiquing conclusions, developing and using models, and obtaining, evaluating, and communicating information.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the organization of the solar system
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the solar system is organized and the various bodies in the solar system interact. Key ideas include matter is distributed throughout the solar system; planets have different sizes and orbit at different distances from the sun; gravity keeps the planets in orbit around the sun; and the solar system formed from a cloud of gas and dust.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the sun-Earth-moon system
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that the sun-Earth-moon system has predictable characteristics and patterns. Key ideas include the Earth rotates on its axis and revolves around the sun; there is a relationship among Earth, the moon, and the sun that causes phases of the moon; and there is a relationship that results in seasons, eclipses, and tides.

standards.description: Investigate and understand energy sources and transformations
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that energy is essential for all life and drives important processes on Earth. Key ideas include the sun is important in driving energy-transforming processes on the surface of Earth and in the atmosphere; energy from the sun is important in atmospheric and environmental conditions; and living systems on Earth depend on energy from the sun.

standards.description: Investigate and understand that all matter is composed of atoms
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that all matter is composed of atoms. Key ideas include atoms have a basic structure; elements can be represented by chemical symbols; and two or more atoms interact to form new substances, which are held together by electrical forces.

standards.description: Investigate and understand the unique properties of water
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that water has unique properties and plays a vital role in the natural and human-made environment. Key ideas include water is essential for living organisms; water has properties that are important for sustaining life; and water has an important role in weathering.

standards.description: Investigate and understand air properties and atmosphere structure
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that air has properties and that Earth's atmosphere has structure and is dynamic. Key ideas include air is a mixture of gaseous elements and compounds; the atmosphere has layers with distinct properties and functions; and changes in the atmosphere affect weather conditions.

standards.description: Investigate and understand watershed systems
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that land and water interact. Key ideas include Virginia's watershed systems are part of larger systems; the health of the Chesapeake Bay is affected by many factors; and natural events and human activities influence the function of watershed systems.

standards.description: Investigate and understand human impact on the environment and public policy
standards.subject: Science
standards.full_text: The student will investigate and understand that humans impact the environment and individuals can influence public policy decisions relating to energy and the environment. Key ideas include management of renewable and nonrenewable resources; mitigation of land-use and environmental hazards through prevention, planning, and technology; and cost/benefit tradeoffs in conservation policies.
